provenance.text: Unity 6 View layer for Favor of the Pharaoh. Implements IPlayerInput via async UI interactions. Reuses Tom's original art assets from Assets/. Top-down board camera. Scene-per-screen. Deterministic dice (engine rolls, Unity animates). Unity 6.3 at C:\Program Files\Unity\Hub\Editor\6000.3.6f1. References FotP.Engine.dll built from src/FotP.Engine/.
provenance.title: work:FotP-UnityView

provenance.text: Fixed 9 of 10 ability implementations that were incorrect vs rules; ShipCaptain was already correct. Changes: (1) Priest: was AllLocked/tokens, now AfterRoll reroll-1-die+pip-on-another; (2) Priestess: was reroll-all, now reroll-1+-chosen AND +1 pip on 1 die; (3) Embalmer: was lock-existing-die, now adds new temp Standard die at value 6 to Active zone; (4) Charioteer: was 1 Standard die, now 2 Standard dice; (5) BadOmen: was take-token, now sets StandardDiceModifierNextTurn; (6) TombBuilder: was +1 pip, now set-value+lock; (7) HeadServant: was up-to-2-dice, now any number; (8) Astrologer: was IsPerRoll, now IsPerTurn; (9) GrainTrader: was StartOfTurn tokens, now AfterRoll +1 pip. Added Player.StandardDiceModifierNextTurn, fixed TurnStateMachine all-locked-after-AfterRoll detection. 22 new unit tests in Level5Level6AbilityTests.cs. All 301 tests pass.
provenance.title: child-done:done:spot-check-10-ability-implementations-against-rules

semanticPayload.summary: work:Create Unity 6 project and import engine DLL

provenance.text: Create a new Unity 6 project under the repo (e.g. unity/ directory). Use URP (Universal Render Pipeline) for modern rendering with 2D/3D flexibility. Import FotP.Engine.dll as a managed plugin. Verify the engine DLL loads and GameState can be instantiated from a MonoBehaviour. Copy Tom's art assets from the old Assets/Textures/ and Assets/Materials/ into the new project. Set up 3 scenes: MainMenu, Game, Results. Unity path: C:\Program Files\Unity\Hub\Editor\6000.3.6f1\Editor. NOTE: Claude cannot run Unity Editor directly. Create the project structure, scripts, and scene configs as files. The user will open in Unity to verify.
provenance.title: work:Create Unity 6 project and import engine DLL

provenance.text: Implements IPlayerInput where each method blocks (via async/Task or coroutine) until the player makes a UI choice. Pattern for each method: 1. Show relevant UI (highlight clickable dice, show buttons, etc). 2. Set a TaskCompletionSource<T> or callback. 3. Await player click/selection. 4. Hide UI, return the selected value. Methods to implement (all async): - ChooseDiceToLock: highlight active dice, player clicks to toggle, Done button - ChooseContinueRolling: show Roll Again / Stop buttons - ChooseTileToClaim: highlight claimable market tiles, click or Pass - ChooseDie: highlight candidate dice, click one - ChooseMultipleDice: highlight candidates, toggle select, Done - ChoosePipValue: number picker (1-6) popup - ChooseScarab: show scarab panel, click one or Pass - ChoosePlayer: show player portraits, click one - ChooseYesNo: Yes/No dialog - ChooseUseAbility: tile glow + click or Skip File: unity/Assets/Scripts/UI/UnityPlayerInput.cs. This is the most critical piece â€” every player interaction flows through it.
provenance.title: work:UnityPlayerInput async IPlayerInput implementation

semanticPayload.summary: work:DiceView - cup, active area, locked pyramid

provenance.text:
Three zones rendered on the board: - Cup area: shows dice count, click to roll - Active area: rolled dice displayed with face values, clickable for
  locking and ability targeting
- Locked area: pyramid arrangement of locked dice Dice rolling animation: when engine rolls, animate the die spinning and landing on the determined value. Can be simple 2D flip animation or 3D tumble â€” start simple (2D sprite swap with a brief shake). Use Tom's dice textures from Assets/Dice/ and Assets/Textures/. Subscribe to zone SmartList events (OnAdded/OnRemoved) to move dice visuals between areas. File: unity/Assets/Scripts/Views/DiceView.cs.
provenance.title: work:DiceView - cup, active area, locked pyramid

provenance.text: UI overlay showing: - Current player indicator and turn phase - Owned tiles bar (scrollable, shows tile abilities) - Scarab token display (reroll/pip/die counts) - Status text for prompts ("Choose a die to lock", etc) - Done/Cancel buttons for multi-step selections - Score display (pyramid total) Use Unity UI Toolkit or legacy Canvas â€” Canvas is simpler for a first pass. Player tiles use Tom's artwork. File: unity/Assets/Scripts/Views/PlayerUI.cs.
provenance.title: work:Player UI panels

provenance.text: Simple menu scene: - Game title (Favor of the Pharaoh) - Player count selection (2-4) - Player name entry (with AI toggle per player) - Bar side selection (A or B) - Random tiles toggle - Play button â†’ loads Game scene with config Use ScriptableObject or static config class to pass settings between scenes. File: unity/Assets/Scripts/UI/MainMenuController.cs.

semanticPayload.summary: work:Integration test - play a full game in Unity

provenance.text: Wire everything together and verify a complete game can be played: 1. Start from Main Menu, configure 2 players (1 human, 1 AI) 2. Play through several turns: roll dice, lock, claim tiles 3. Verify scarab spending works 4. Verify tile abilities trigger and prompt correctly 5. Play to roll-off and verify winner is displayed This is a manual test â€” document the steps for the user to verify in the Unity Editor play mode. File: unity/Assets/Scripts/Tests/IntegrationTestChecklist.md.
provenance.title: work:Integration test - play a full game in Unity

semanticPayload.summary: work:BoardView - market bars and tile display

provenance.text: Top-down board layout showing: - 5 level bars (levels 3-7) arranged vertically - Each bar has slots with tile stacks (use Tom's tile textures) - Tile stacks show top tile face, count indicator for remaining - Empty slots grayed out - Claimable tiles highlight when in Claim phase Subscribe to GameController events to update when tiles are claimed. Use Tom's textures from Assets/Textures/ for tile art. File: unity/Assets/Scripts/Views/BoardView.cs.
provenance.title: work:BoardView - market bars and tile display

provenance.text: Central MonoBehaviour that owns GameState and GameRunner. Lives in the Game scene. On scene load: creates GameState, calls Setup() with player configs, creates GameRunner. Runs the game loop via async/coroutine â€” GameRunner.RunGame() is synchronous so wrap each turn step in a coroutine yield or use UniTask for async. Exposes events that Views subscribe to: OnTurnStarted, OnDiceRolled, OnDiceLocked, OnTileClaimed, OnPhaseChanged, OnGameOver. Holds reference to UnityPlayerInput. File: unity/Assets/Scripts/GameController.cs.
provenance.title: work:GameController MonoBehaviour
